select-all.label: Selecting All Columns
select-all.summary: Use the wildcard (*) to select all columns.

sketch-what-is-select-all.title: What Does SELECT * Do?
sketch-what-is-select-all.bodyMarkdown: In SQL, you can use the asterisk (*) as a wildcard to select all columns from a table. This means you don't have to list each column name individually. For example, `SELECT * FROM products;` retrieves every column for every row in the `products` table.

sketch-when-to-use-select-all.title: When Should You Use SELECT *?
sketch-when-to-use-select-all.bodyMarkdown: Using `SELECT *` is convenient when you want to quickly view all the data in a table, especially during exploration or debugging. However, in production queries, it's often better to specify only the columns you need for performance and clarity.

quiz-single-choice-select-all.title: Purpose of SELECT *
quiz-single-choice-select-all.prompt: What does the SQL statement `SELECT * FROM products;` do?
quiz-single-choice-select-all.hint: Think about what the * symbol means in SQL.

help.concept: The * symbol in SQL is a wildcard that represents all columns in a table.
help.hint_1: The query retrieves every column for each row in the table.
help.hint_2: It does not filter or delete data; it simply shows all columns.

options.a: Selects all columns from the products table
options.b: Selects only the first column from the products table
options.c: Deletes all rows from the products table
options.d: Selects only rows where the value is *

quiz-multi-choice-select-all-usage.title: Choosing When to Use SELECT *
quiz-multi-choice-select-all-usage.prompt: In which situations is it appropriate to use `SELECT *`? (Choose all that apply)
quiz-multi-choice-select-all-usage.hint: Consider when you need all columns versus just a few.

help.concept: SELECT * is best for exploration or debugging, not for optimized queries.
help.hint_1: It's useful when you want to see everything in a table.
help.hint_2: For production or performance, it's better to specify columns.

options.a: When quickly exploring a new table
options.b: When you only need a few specific columns
options.d: When optimizing for performance in production

quiz-drag-reorder-select-all.title: Order the Steps: Selecting All Columns
quiz-drag-reorder-select-all.prompt: Arrange the steps to write a SQL query that selects all columns from the `products` table.
quiz-drag-reorder-select-all.hint: Start with the SQL keyword, then indicate all columns, then the table.

help.concept: A SQL SELECT statement follows a specific order: SELECT, columns, FROM, table, semicolon.
help.hint_1: Begin with SELECT, then use * for all columns.
help.hint_2: Don't forget to specify the table and end the statement properly.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-all.title: Fill in the Blank: SELECT All Syntax
quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-all.prompt:
Fill in the blank to select all columns from the `products` table:

SELECT ___ FROM products;
quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-all.hint: Which symbol is used as a wildcard for all columns?

help.concept: The asterisk (*) is the SQL wildcard for all columns.
help.hint_1: It's a single character, not a word.
help.hint_2: It's the same symbol used for multiplication in math.

quiz-code-input-select-all.title: Write a Query: Select All Columns
quiz-code-input-select-all.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the `products` table.
quiz-code-input-select-all.hint: Use SELECT, the wildcard, and specify the table.

help.concept: To select all columns, use SELECT *, then specify the table name.
help.hint_2: Don't forget the FROM clause and the table name.
